name: Veeva Vault API
what_it_does: Veeva Vault REST API for cloud-based content management and collaboration platform for life sciences. Enables AI agents to manage regulatory submissions, clinical documents, quality records, and commercial content; retrieve document metadata and status; automate workflows; and integrate Veeva Vault into life sciences operations. Veeva is the dominant cloud platform for pharmaceutical and biotech companies.
use_cases: Retrieving regulatory submission document status from compliance monitoring agents
Managing clinical trial documents from clinical operations workflow agents
Accessing quality management records from QMS compliance agents
Integrating Veeva Vault content status into regulatory reporting workflow agents
not_for: Non-life sciences industries (Veeva is pharma/biotech focused)
best_when: A pharmaceutical or biotech company using Veeva Vault wants AI agents to automate document workflows, track regulatory submission status, and integrate content management data into operations.
avoid_when: AUTHORIZED USE ONLY: Regulatory submission documents and clinical data are regulated content. Access must comply with FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GxP, and applicable regulatory frameworks. Unauthorized modifications can be a regulatory violation.

auth.notes: Veeva Vault API supports username/password auth to generate session tokens, API key auth (OAuth 2.0 with JWT bearer), and Veeva OpenID Connect. Session tokens are short-lived. GxP audit trails require proper session attribution.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise life sciences platform with modular Vault pricing
pricing.notes: Enterprise-only platform for life sciences. Pricing per vault type and user count. Significant enterprise investment.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per API client; documented in developer portal (burst and sustained limits)

known_agent_gotchas: AUTHORIZED USE ONLY: Regulatory and clinical documents are regulated content — FDA 21 CFR Part 11 compliance required
No public MCP server — REST API with complex session token management
GxP audit trail requirements — all API actions must be attributed to identified users
Vault types (eTMF, RIM, Quality, PromoMats, etc.) have different data models and workflows
Session tokens expire after 20 minutes of inactivity — implement refresh logic
Veeva's developer sandbox requires separate provisioning — contact Veeva for developer access
agent_readiness.security_notes: AUTHORIZED USE ONLY: Regulated life sciences content (FDA 21 CFR Part 11, GxP). SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2/JWT. Regulatory audit trail requirements. EU data residency.

security_score_components.security_notes: Regulated pharmaceutical content. 21 CFR Part 11, GxP, SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2/JWT. GxP audit trail. EU data residency.
